prompt:
Declaration (in builder format):

project.new("webby", "websocket multiplayer game")
  .players(capacity: 2-8, states: ["lobby", "in_game", "spectator"], auth: "ephemeral_token")
  .rounds(duration: "60s", win: "first_to_10")
  .protocol(transport: "websocket", serialize: "msgpack", heartbeat: "5s")
  .persistence(leaderboards: "redis", match_history: "postgres")
  .deploy(env: ["staging", "prod"], region: "auto")

Output a flat bullet list manifest of every concrete element this declaration implies.
manifest:
- Project name: webby
- Project type: websocket multiplayer game
- Minimum player capacity: 2
- Maximum player capacity: 8
- Player state: lobby
- Player state: in_game
- Player state: spectator
- Authentication method: ephemeral token
- Ephemeral token must be generated per session/connection
- Ephemeral token must be validated on connection
- Ephemeral token must be invalidated on disconnect or session end
- Round duration: 60 seconds
- Round timer must start on round begin
- Round timer must enforce end-of-round at 60 seconds
- Win condition: first player/team to reach 10 points
- Score must be tracked per player per match
- Win condition check must be evaluated after every score event
- Transport protocol: WebSocket
- Serialization format: MessagePack
- All messages must be serialized with MessagePack
- All messages must be deserialized with MessagePack
- Heartbeat interval: 5 seconds
- Server must send or expect heartbeat every 5 seconds
- Connections failing heartbeat must be dropped or flagged
- Leaderboard persistence store: Redis
- Match history persistence store: PostgreSQL
- Leaderboard read/write operations target Redis
- Match history read/write operations target PostgreSQL
- Match history schema must be defined in PostgreSQL
- Deployment environment: staging
- Deployment environment: prod
- Staging and prod are separate deployment targets
- Deployment region: auto-selected
- Region selection is automatic (not manually specified)
- Both staging and prod deployments use auto region selection
- Player state transitions must be managed (lobby → in_game, in_game → spectator, etc.)
- Server must support 2 to 8 concurrent players per match instance
- Match result must be written to match history (PostgreSQL) on game end
- Leaderboard must be updated in Redis on game end
- WebSocket server must handle concurrent connections up to player capacity
